title: Introduction to the Special Issue on Digital Touch
summary: This paper frames digital touch as a communicative, relational, social, and cultural practice rather than merely a sensory or physiological phenomenon. It expands the discourse of touch beyond device-centric or implementation-focused views. It is useful as a conceptual anchor for understanding technologies of touch as a broader field.

related.reason: Both expand touch beyond a narrow technical framing.
Both treat touch as communicative and relational.
Both resist reducing touch to neutral input.
Both help define the broader field of digital and mediated touch.

notes: This functions almost like a manifesto for expanded theorization of touch. It is especially useful for framing touch as a practice rather than an input modality.
quotes: Digital touch reshapes who can touch, what can be touched, and how touch is experienced across distance and mediation.
